Divide the canvas into cells, one per input point. Each cell contains every location that is closer to its seed point than to any other seed. Returns a list of closed regions in input order, clipped to the sewable field (or a given region).

Common uses: organic tiling, stipple shading, cell-based fill patterns, or growing a motif inside each natural territory.

Max 10,000 input points.

```
let seeds = scatter(10)
let cells = voronoi(seeds)
for cell in cells [
  beginfill
    sewpath(resample(cell, 2))
  endfill  trim
]
```

features.label: triangulate
features.summary: Delaunay triangulation: connects a set of points into triangles such that the circumcircle of each triangle contains no other point. Returns a list of 3-point regions. The "dual" of Voronoi — the same seeds that define Voronoi cells also define the triangle mesh connecting them.

editor.documentation:
Delaunay triangulation: connects a set of points into triangles such that the circumcircle of each triangle contains no other point. Returns a list of 3-point regions. The "dual" of Voronoi — the same seeds that define Voronoi cells also define the triangle mesh connecting them.

Common uses: structural weaving patterns (sew along each triangle edge), mesh-based fill, or truss-like geometric motifs.

Max 10,000 input points.

```
let pts = scatter(14)
let tris = triangulate(pts)
for tri in tris [
  up  setpos(tri[0])  down
  sewpath(tri)
  setpos(tri[0])  up  trim
]
```

features.label: hull
features.summary: Convex hull: the smallest convex polygon that encloses all given points, returned as a counter-clockwise region. Think of it as wrapping a rubber band around all the points — only the outermost ones form the boundary.

editor.documentation:
Convex hull: the smallest convex polygon that encloses all given points, returned as a counter-clockwise region. Think of it as wrapping a rubber band around all the points — only the outermost ones form the boundary.

Use it as a bounding region for scatter or fill, to outline a cluster of points, or as a clip region.

```
let pts = scatter(5)
let outline = hull(pts)
beginfill
  sewpath(resample(outline, 2))
endfill  trim
```

features.label: relax
features.summary: n rounds of Lloyd's relaxation — moves each point to its Voronoi cell's centroid for even stippling.

editor.documentation: n rounds of Lloyd's relaxation — moves each point to its Voronoi cell's centroid for even stippling.

features.label: offsetpath
features.summary: Inflate (+) or shrink (−) a region. Returns a list of regions. Shrinking may split or erase the shape entirely.

editor.documentation: Inflate (+) or shrink (−) a region. Returns a list of regions. Shrinking may split or erase the shape entirely.

features.label: contourpaths
features.summary: Closed inset contours at half-gap then gap spacing, ordered outside-in.

editor.documentation: Closed inset contours at half-gap then gap spacing, ordered outside-in.

features.label: spiralpath
features.summary: Contour rings spliced into one open inward path per disconnected fragment.

editor.documentation: Contour rings spliced into one open inward path per disconnected fragment.

features.label: fillrows
features.summary: Routed, unsplit tatami rows without pull compensation, ready for `fill paths`.

editor.documentation: Routed, unsplit tatami rows without pull compensation, ready for `fill paths`.

features.label: closepath
features.summary: Return the ring with its first point repeated. Requires at least three points.

editor.documentation: Return the ring with its first point repeated. Requires at least three points.

features.label: clippaths
features.summary: Boolean operation on two regions. Backed by Clipper2 at μm precision. Returns a list of regions.

editor.documentation:
Boolean operation on two regions. Backed by Clipper2 at μm precision. Returns a list of regions.

Operations: `'union'` `'intersect'` `'difference'` `'xor'`

features.label: inpath
features.summary: 1 if the point is inside the region (even-odd rule, consistent with fills).

editor.documentation: 1 if the point is inside the region (even-odd rule, consistent with fills).

features.label: infield
features.summary: `1` if the point is inside the current sewable field, `0` otherwise. The point is mapped through the current transform (local frame → hoop space), consistent with `coverat`. Zero RNG draws.

editor.detail: 1 if point is inside the sewable field
editor.documentation:
`1` if the point is inside the current sewable field, `0` otherwise. The point is mapped through the current transform (local frame → hoop space), consistent with `coverat`. Zero RNG draws.

```
if infield(pos()) [ fd 2 ]  // only sew if inside the field
```

features.label: fieldbounds
features.summary: Returns `[minX, minY, maxX, maxY]` — the bounding box of the sewable field in hoop space (mm). Same format as `bbox()`. Zero RNG draws.

editor.detail: bounding box of the sewable field
editor.documentation:
Returns `[minX, minY, maxX, maxY]` — the bounding box of the sewable field in hoop space (mm). Same format as `bbox()`. Zero RNG draws.

```
let b = fieldbounds()  // e.g. [-47, -47, 47, 47] for round100
```

features.label: fieldpath
features.summary: Returns the boundary of the sewable field as a counter-clockwise polygon, ready for use as a region in `scatter`, `clippaths`, `offsetpath`, etc. Round fields are polygonised at ≤ 2 mm chords. Zero RNG draws.

editor.detail: sewable field boundary as a CCW region
editor.documentation:
Returns the boundary of the sewable field as a counter-clockwise polygon, ready for use as a region in `scatter`, `clippaths`, `offsetpath`, etc. Round fields are polygonised at ≤ 2 mm chords. Zero RNG draws.

`offsetpath(fieldpath(), -5)` gives a 5 mm safety margin inside whatever hoop is configured.

```
hoop '5x7'
let margin = first(offsetpath(fieldpath(), -6))
let pts = scatter(5, margin)
```

features.label: xlate
features.summary: New path shifted by `(dx, dy)` mm. The functional companion to the `translate` block command — composes with `scatter`/`voronoi`/`offsetpath` data.

editor.documentation: New path shifted by `(dx, dy)` mm. The functional companion to the `translate` block command — composes with `scatter`/`voronoi`/`offsetpath` data.

features.label: xrotate
features.summary: New path rotated `deg` clockwise. Optional pivot: `xrotate(path, deg, cx, cy)`.

editor.documentation: New path rotated `deg` clockwise. Optional pivot: `xrotate(path, deg, cx, cy)`.

features.label: xscale
features.summary: New path scaled by `sx` (and `sy`). `xscale(path, s)` is uniform; `xscale(path, sx, sy)` is per-axis.

editor.documentation: New path scaled by `sx` (and `sy`). `xscale(path, s)` is uniform; `xscale(path, sx, sy)` is per-axis.

features.label: xmirror
features.summary: New path reflected across a line through the origin at heading `deg`.

editor.documentation: New path reflected across a line through the origin at heading `deg`.

features.label: warppath
features.summary: New path with every point mapped through a `@name` reporter — the functional companion to the `warp` block. `warp @f [ sewpath(P) ]` ≡ `sewpath(warppath(P, @f))`.

editor.detail: map a path through a reporter (pure)
editor.documentation: New path with every point mapped through a `@name` reporter — the functional companion to the `warp` block. `warp @f [ sewpath(P) ]` ≡ `sewpath(warppath(P, @f))`.

features.label: humanizepath
features.summary: New path with seeded coherent jitter (`amount` mm) — the functional companion to `humanize`. Forks one draw from the seeded stream.

editor.detail: seeded coherent jitter on a path (pure)
editor.documentation:
New path with seeded coherent jitter (`amount` mm) — the functional companion to `humanize`. Forks one draw from the seeded stream.

```
let coast = humanizepath(resample(cell, 2.0), 0.3)
sewpath(coast)
```

features.label: snappath
features.summary: New path with every point snapped to the fixed lattice — the functional companion to `snaptogrid`, same arity overloads (cell | cellx celly | …ox oy | …ang).

editor.detail: quantize a path to a fixed lattice (pure)
editor.documentation:
New path with every point snapped to the fixed lattice — the functional companion to `snaptogrid`, same arity overloads (cell | cellx celly | …ox oy | …ang).

```
let pts = snappath(scatter(8), 2)   // Poisson points on a 2 mm grid
```

features.label: declumppath
features.summary: Run the `declump` fold over an explicit point list, reading real committed coverage history but committing nothing — the pure data twin of `declump`. Drawless.

editor.detail: along-axis crowd relief on a path (pure, read-only)
editor.documentation:
Run the `declump` fold over an explicit point list, reading real committed coverage history but committing nothing — the pure data twin of `declump`. Drawless.

The fold self-interacts exactly as the block form does (consecutive points see each other's moved positions), but the results are never fed to the density grid, so `coverat` is unchanged after the call.

**Important:** resample to stitch pitch first, then sew:

```
sewpath(declumppath(resample(spine, 2.5), 2, 1.5))
```

Args: `declumppath(path, limit)` or `declumppath(path, limit, maxshift)` — same units as the block form.

features.label: satinpair
features.summary: Build the 5-slot satin reporter contract by intent.

editor.documentation:
Build the 5-slot satin reporter contract by intent.

`satinpair(advance, width)` → `[advance, width, width, 0, 0]`

The common case: a symmetric perpendicular bite of the given width. Equivalent to the built-in `satin` generator.

Draw cost: 0. Library tier — shadowable with a note.

```
def leaf(t, s, i, u) [
  return satinpair(0.45, sin(s * 180) * 2.2)
]
```

features.label: satinrake
features.summary: Build the 5-slot satin reporter contract by intent.

editor.detail: raked satin tuple: [adv, w, w, -lag, lag]
editor.documentation:
Build the 5-slot satin reporter contract by intent.

`satinrake(advance, width, lag)` → `[advance, width, width, -lag, lag]`

Rakes the stitch into a diagonal by `lag` mm. Alternating the sign each pair makes successive diagonals cross — woven / crosshatch satin.

Draw cost: 0. Library tier — shadowable with a note.

```
def crosshatch(t, s, i, u) [
  if mod(i, 2) = 0 [ return satinrake(0.4, 2, 0.8) ]
  return satinrake(0.4, 2, -0.8)
]
```

features.label: satinasym
features.summary: Build the 5-slot satin reporter contract by intent.

editor.documentation:
Build the 5-slot satin reporter contract by intent.

`satinasym(advance, leftw, rightw)` → `[advance, leftw, rightw, 0, 0]`

Asymmetric column: left and right rail widths are different, no rake.

Draw cost: 0. Library tier — shadowable with a note.

features.label: railinset
features.summary: `railinset(advance, inset)` builds `[advance, inset, inset, 0, 0]` for a `satinbetween` shape reporter. Insets move inward from both authored rails. Pure, drawless, Library tier.

editor.documentation: `railinset(advance, inset)` builds `[advance, inset, inset, 0, 0]` for a `satinbetween` shape reporter. Insets move inward from both authored rails. Pure, drawless, Library tier.

features.label: railrake
features.summary: `railrake(advance, lag)` builds `[advance, 0, 0, -lag, lag]` for a full-width raked `satinbetween` stitch. Pure, drawless, Library tier.

editor.documentation: `railrake(advance, lag)` builds `[advance, 0, 0, -lag, lag]` for a full-width raked `satinbetween` stitch. Pure, drawless, Library tier.

features.label: railspine
features.summary: Returns the same derived midpoint path used by `satinbetween`, including orientation and deterministic closed-rail seam handling. Useful for a centre vein or manual run. Pure and drawless.

editor.detail: derived midpoint path between two rails
editor.documentation: Returns the same derived midpoint path used by `satinbetween`, including orientation and deterministic closed-rail seam handling. Useful for a centre vein or manual run. Pure and drawless.

features.label: tatamirow
features.summary: Build the 3-slot fill shape reporter contract by intent.

editor.documentation:
Build the 3-slot fill shape reporter contract by intent.

`tatamirow(spacing, len)` → `[spacing, len, 0.5]` — standard brick offset
`tatamirow(spacing, len, phase)` → `[spacing, len, phase]` — explicit phase

Used inside a `fill shape @fn` reporter to return the row descriptor without memorising slot order. `phase = 0.5` is the standard tatami brick offset.

Draw cost: 0. Library tier — shadowable with a note.

```
def thin(p, row, v) [
  return tatamirow(remap(v, 0, 1, 0.4, 1.1), 2.5)
]
fill shape @thin
```

features.label: coverat
features.summary: Coverage at a point, in layers (the heatmap / `maxdensity` unit; 1 ≈ one clean satin/tatami pass), read live and in sewing order over everything committed so far.

editor.detail: thread coverage in layers at a point (live, pure)
editor.documentation:
Coverage at a point, in **layers** (the heatmap / `maxdensity` unit; 1 ≈ one clean satin/tatami pass), read live and in sewing order over everything committed so far.

`coverat(p)` — the containing 1 mm cell
`coverat(p, r)` — averaged over radius `r` mm

Pure: zero RNG draws, draws nothing. Sees flushed penetrations (a buffered satin column isn’t visible until it ends).

```
if coverat(p) < 1.5 [ up setpos(p) down arc 360 0.5 trim ]
```

features.label: countat
features.summary: The number of penetrations in the 1 mm cell containing `p`, read live. Pure: zero draws, draws nothing.

editor.detail: penetration count at a point (live, pure)
editor.documentation: The number of penetrations in the 1 mm cell containing `p`, read live. Pure: zero draws, draws nothing.

features.label: nearestsewn
features.summary: The closest already-sewn penetration to `p`, as `[x, y]` in hoop space, or `[]` if nothing is sewn yet. Backed by a spatial index, so it stays O(local) — no history scan. Pure: zero draws.

editor.detail: closest prior penetration to a point (or [])
editor.documentation: The closest already-sewn penetration to `p`, as `[x, y]` in hoop space, or `[]` if nothing is sewn yet. Backed by a spatial index, so it stays O(local) — no history scan. Pure: zero draws.

features.label: sewnwithin
features.summary: A list of already-sewn penetrations within `r` mm of `p` (hoop space). Grid-bucketed, so proximity logic stays O(local) instead of scanning the whole history.

editor.detail: prior penetrations within r mm of a point
editor.documentation:
A list of already-sewn penetrations within `r` mm of `p` (hoop space). Grid-bucketed, so proximity logic stays O(local) instead of scanning the whole history.

```
if len(sewnwithin(p, 2)) = 0 [ … ]   // nothing crowding p yet
```

Pure: zero draws.

features.label: stitchedpoints
features.summary: A deep-copied list of every penetration committed so far, as a path of `[x, y]` points (hoop space), captured at call time. Explicit and opt-in: you pay the O(n) copy when you ask, and the result is just a list (safe to mutate). Pure: zero draws.

editor.detail: snapshot: a deep copy of all penetrations so far
editor.documentation: A deep-copied list of every penetration committed so far, as a path of `[x, y]` points (hoop space), captured at call time. Explicit and opt-in: you pay the O(n) copy when you ask, and the result is just a list (safe to mutate). Pure: zero draws.

features.label: backward
features.summary: Alias for `bk`. Sew backward n mm.

editor.documentation:
Alias for `bk`. Sew backward n mm.

Aliases: `back`, `backward`

features.label: pu
features.summary: Alias for `up`. Needle up — subsequent moves are jump travels, not stitches.

editor.detail: pen up (travel mode) — alias for up
editor.documentation:
Alias for `up`. Needle up — subsequent moves are jump travels, not stitches.

Aliases: `penup`, `pu`

features.label: pd
features.summary: Alias for `down`. Needle down — subsequent moves sew stitches.

editor.detail: pen down (sew mode) — alias for down
editor.documentation:
Alias for `down`. Needle down — subsequent moves sew stitches.

Aliases: `pendown`, `pd`

features.label: clearscreen
features.summary: Alias for `cs`. Accepted for Logo familiarity; does nothing in NeedleScript.

editor.documentation:
Alias for `cs`. Accepted for Logo familiarity; does nothing in NeedleScript.

Aliases: `clearscreen`, `clear`

features.label: clear
features.summary: Alias for `cs`. Accepted for Logo familiarity; does nothing in NeedleScript.

editor.documentation:
Alias for `cs`. Accepted for Logo familiarity; does nothing in NeedleScript.

Aliases: `clearscreen`, `clear`

features.label: rgb
features.summary: Return a normalized hex color from red, green, and blue channels in 0…1. Values outside the range are clamped. Pure and drawless.

editor.documentation: Return a normalized hex color from red, green, and blue channels in 0…1. Values outside the range are clamped. Pure and drawless.

features.label: hsl
features.summary: Return a normalized hex color from hue in degrees plus saturation and lightness in 0…1. Hue wraps; saturation and lightness clamp. Pure and drawless.

editor.documentation: Return a normalized hex color from hue in degrees plus saturation and lightness in 0…1. Hue wraps; saturation and lightness clamp. Pure and drawless.

features.label: hexparts
features.summary: Parse a supported color string and return normalized `[r, g, b]` channels in 0…1. Pure and drawless.

editor.documentation: Parse a supported color string and return normalized `[r, g, b]` channels in 0…1. Pure and drawless.

features.label: lerpcolor
features.summary: Interpolate colors at unclamped `t`. The default mode is perceptual OKLab; pass `'rgb'` as a fourth argument for raw sRGB interpolation. Returns a normalized hex color. Pure and drawless.

editor.documentation: Interpolate colors at unclamped `t`. The default mode is perceptual OKLab; pass `'rgb'` as a fourth argument for raw sRGB interpolation. Returns a normalized hex color. Pure and drawless.

features.label: nearestcolor
features.summary: Return the lowest-index color in a non-empty palette with the smallest perceptual OKLab distance from `color`. Pure and drawless.

editor.documentation: Return the lowest-index color in a non-empty palette with the smallest perceptual OKLab distance from `color`. Pure and drawless.

features.label: colordist
features.summary: Return the OKLab distance between two supported color strings. Smaller values are more visually similar. Pure and drawless.

editor.documentation: Return the OKLab distance between two supported color strings. Smaller values are more visually similar. Pure and drawless.

features.label: slotcolor
features.summary: Return the normalized hex color for a 1-based palette slot, including the deterministic default color for an undeclared slot. Reads metadata, emits nothing, and draws nothing.

editor.documentation: Return the normalized hex color for a 1-based palette slot, including the deterministic default color for an undeclared slot. Reads metadata, emits nothing, and draws nothing.

features.label: colorindex
features.summary: Return the active thread slot as a 1-based index. Reads machine state, emits nothing, and draws nothing.

editor.documentation: Return the active thread slot as a 1-based index. Reads machine state, emits nothing, and draws nothing.

features.label: colorhex
features.summary: Return the normalized hex color of the active thread slot. Reads palette metadata, emits nothing, and draws nothing.

editor.documentation: Return the normalized hex color of the active thread slot. Reads palette metadata, emits nothing, and draws nothing.

features.label: backgroundcolor
features.summary: Return the normalized resolved background color. Reads design metadata, emits nothing, and draws nothing.

editor.documentation: Return the normalized resolved background color. Reads design metadata, emits nothing, and draws nothing.

features.label: curveflat
features.summary: Adaptively flatten editable cubic anchors into a path at `tolerance` millimetres. Relative handles and compact corner anchors are supported; optional mode `'closed'` closes the curve. Pure and drawless.

editor.detail: flatten an editable cubic curve spec
editor.documentation: Adaptively flatten editable cubic anchors into a path at `tolerance` millimetres. Relative handles and compact corner anchors are supported; optional mode `'closed'` closes the curve. Pure and drawless.

features.label: curvepath
features.summary: Flatten an editable cubic curve spec at 0.05 mm tolerance, then arc-length resample it with numeric, list, or reporter spacing. Optional phase and `'open'`/`'closed'` mode follow `resample` semantics.

editor.detail: flatten and resample a curve spec
editor.documentation: Flatten an editable cubic curve spec at 0.05 mm tolerance, then arc-length resample it with numeric, list, or reporter spacing. Optional phase and `'open'`/`'closed'` mode follow `resample` semantics.

features.label: isclosed
features.summary: Return `1` when a path explicitly repeats its first point at the end, otherwise `0`. Non-empty path validation still applies. Pure and drawless.

editor.documentation: Return `1` when a path explicitly repeats its first point at the end, otherwise `0`. Non-empty path validation still applies. Pure and drawless.

features.label: openpath
features.summary: Return a new open path by removing a duplicate final point when the input is canonically closed. Other vertices are preserved. Pure and drawless.

editor.documentation: Return a new open path by removing a duplicate final point when the input is canonically closed. Other vertices are preserved. Pure and drawless.

features.label: pathorientation
features.summary: Return `1` for counter-clockwise, `-1` for clockwise, or `0` for a degenerate path, using the implicit closing segment. Pure and drawless.

editor.documentation: Return `1` for counter-clockwise, `-1` for clockwise, or `0` for a degenerate path, using the implicit closing segment. Pure and drawless.

features.label: pointat
features.summary: Return the point at normalized arc-length parameter `t` on an open path. Parameters are clamped to 0…1. Pure and drawless.

editor.documentation: Return the point at normalized arc-length parameter `t` on an open path. Parameters are clamped to 0…1. Pure and drawless.

features.label: headingat
features.summary: Return the turtle heading of an open path at normalized arc-length parameter `t`. Parameters are clamped to 0…1. Pure and drawless.

editor.documentation: Return the turtle heading of an open path at normalized arc-length parameter `t`. Parameters are clamped to 0…1. Pure and drawless.

features.label: normalat
features.summary: Return the turtle heading of the normal pointing left of path travel at normalized arc-length parameter `t`. Pure and drawless.

editor.detail: left normal heading on a path
editor.documentation: Return the turtle heading of the normal pointing left of path travel at normalized arc-length parameter `t`. Pure and drawless.

features.label: paramof
features.summary: Project a point to the nearest location on an open path and return its normalized arc-length parameter in 0…1. Pure and drawless.

editor.documentation: Project a point to the nearest location on an open path and return its normalized arc-length parameter in 0…1. Pure and drawless.

features.label: paramtomm
features.summary: Convert normalized arc-length parameter `t` to millimetres along a path. The parameter is clamped to 0…1. Pure and drawless.

editor.documentation: Convert normalized arc-length parameter `t` to millimetres along a path. The parameter is clamped to 0…1. Pure and drawless.

features.label: mmtoparam
features.summary: Convert a distance in millimetres along a path to normalized arc-length parameter 0…1. Distance is clamped to the path length. Pure and drawless.

editor.documentation: Convert a distance in millimetres along a path to normalized arc-length parameter 0…1. Distance is clamped to the path length. Pure and drawless.

features.label: subpath
features.summary: Return the shape-preserving open subpath between normalized arc-length parameters `a` and `b`, including interpolated boundary points. Pure and drawless.

editor.documentation: Return the shape-preserving open subpath between normalized arc-length parameters `a` and `b`, including interpolated boundary points. Pure and drawless.

features.label: splitat
features.summary: Return two shape-preserving subpaths split at normalized arc-length parameter `t`. The shared split point ends the first and starts the second. Pure and drawless.

editor.detail: split a path at a parameter
editor.documentation: Return two shape-preserving subpaths split at normalized arc-length parameter `t`. The shared split point ends the first and starts the second. Pure and drawless.

features.label: insertvertex
features.summary: Return a path with a vertex inserted at normalized arc-length parameter `t` without changing the represented polyline shape. Pure and drawless.

editor.detail: insert a vertex at a parameter
editor.documentation: Return a path with a vertex inserted at normalized arc-length parameter `t` without changing the represented polyline shape. Pure and drawless.

features.label: dashes
features.summary: Return open dash fragments using repeating on/off lengths in millimetres. An optional phase enters the cycle; lengths must be non-negative with a positive sum. Pure and drawless.

editor.detail: split a path into dash fragments
editor.documentation: Return open dash fragments using repeating on/off lengths in millimetres. An optional phase enters the cycle; lengths must be non-negative with a positive sum. Pure and drawless.

features.label: pathisectparams
features.summary: Return path intersections as `[point, ta, tb]`, where `ta` and `tb` are normalized arc-length parameters on the two input paths. Pure and drawless.

editor.documentation: Return path intersections as `[point, ta, tb]`, where `ta` and `tb` are normalized arc-length parameters on the two input paths. Pure and drawless.

features.label: pathselfisects
features.summary: Return non-adjacent self-intersections as `[point, ta, tb]` with normalized arc-length parameters on the input path. Pure and drawless.

editor.documentation: Return non-adjacent self-intersections as `[point, ta, tb]` with normalized arc-length parameters on the input path. Pure and drawless.

features.label: joinpaths
features.summary: Deterministically weld fragment endpoints within `tolerance` millimetres. Closed chains become canonical rings; the result is a list of paths. Pure and drawless.

editor.documentation: Deterministically weld fragment endpoints within `tolerance` millimetres. Closed chains become canonical rings; the result is a list of paths. Pure and drawless.

features.label: ispoint
features.summary: Return `1` only for a two-number finite point `[x, y]`; return `0` for every other value without throwing. Pure and drawless.

editor.documentation: Return `1` only for a two-number finite point `[x, y]`; return `0` for every other value without throwing. Pure and drawless.

features.label: ispath
features.summary: Return `1` only for a list of at least two finite points; return `0` for every other value without throwing. Pure and drawless.

editor.documentation: Return `1` only for a list of at least two finite points; return `0` for every other value without throwing. Pure and drawless.

features.label: iscurvespec
features.summary: Return `1` when a value is a valid editable cubic curve specification; return `0` instead of throwing on malformed input. Pure and drawless.

editor.documentation: Return `1` when a value is a valid editable cubic curve specification; return `0` instead of throwing on malformed input. Pure and drawless.

features.label: strokepath
features.summary: Return canonical outline regions for a path stroke of `width` millimetres. Optional caps are `'round'`, `'butt'`, or `'square'`; joins are `'round'`, `'miter'`, or `'bevel'`. Pure and drawless.

editor.documentation: Return canonical outline regions for a path stroke of `width` millimetres. Optional caps are `'round'`, `'butt'`, or `'square'`; joins are `'round'`, `'miter'`, or `'bevel'`. Pure and drawless.

features.label: clipopen
features.summary: Return open fragments of a path inside a compound even-odd region, or outside it when optional mode is `'outside'`. Pure and drawless.

editor.detail: clip an open path to a region
editor.documentation: Return open fragments of a path inside a compound even-odd region, or outside it when optional mode is `'outside'`. Pure and drawless.

modules.title: std.mathx
modules.purpose: Easing, angles, vectors, remapping, and deterministic random helpers
modules.rngDraws: Only randbetween, randint, chance, weightedpick, and jitterpt draw (1, 1, 1, 1, and 2).

modules.title: std.listx
modules.purpose: Sorting, selection, reshaping, and predicate-based list operations
modules.rngDraws: Only when a supplied callback draws.

modules.title: std.shapes
modules.purpose: Centered closed and open path constructors

modules.title: std.regions
modules.purpose: Region measurement, tiling, insets, and partitions
modules.rngDraws: partitions draws exactly 1; all other exports draw none.

modules.title: std.layout
modules.purpose: Point/heading layouts and uniform path fitting

modules.title: std.textures
modules.description: fill callbacks and clipped texture paths
modules.purpose: Direction/shape callbacks and clipped fill paths
modules.rngDraws: None; seeded simplex fields do not advance the main stream.

modules.title: std.stitchcraft
modules.purpose: Reusable embroidery construction and geometry rituals
modules.rngDraws: stipple draws exactly 1; all other exports draw none unless a callback draws.

procedures.name: easein
procedures.summary: Quadratic ease-in, `u²`, after clamping `t` to 0…1.
procedures.documentation: `easein(t) -> number`. Quadratic ease-in, `u²`, after clamping `t` to 0…1.

procedures.name: easeout
procedures.summary: Quadratic ease-out, `1 - (1-u)²`, with clamped input.
procedures.documentation: `easeout(t) -> number`. Quadratic ease-out, `1 - (1-u)²`, with clamped input.

procedures.name: easeinout
procedures.summary: Symmetric quadratic ease-in/out with clamped input; its midpoint is 0.5.
procedures.documentation: `easeinout(t) -> number`. Symmetric quadratic ease-in/out with clamped input; its midpoint is 0.5.

procedures.name: easeback
procedures.summary: Back-ease curve using overshoot constant 1.70158. Input is clamped, but the curve itself dips below 0 near the start.
procedures.documentation: `easeback(t) -> number`. Back-ease curve using overshoot constant 1.70158. Input is clamped, but the curve itself dips below 0 near the start.

procedures.name: easepow
procedures.summary: Returns a configured one-argument reporter equivalent to `pow(clamp(t, 0, 1), power)`. Use directly as `easepow(3)(0.5)` or pass the returned reference to another reporter.
procedures.documentation: `easepow(power) -> reference`. Returns a configured one-argument reporter equivalent to `pow(clamp(t, 0, 1), power)`. Use directly as `easepow(3)(0.5)` or pass the returned reference to another reporter.

procedures.name: triwave
procedures.summary: Period-1 triangle wave: −1 at integer boundaries, 0 at quarter periods, and 1 at half periods. Negative `t` wraps with floor modulo.
procedures.documentation: `triwave(t) -> number`. Period-1 triangle wave: −1 at integer boundaries, 0 at quarter periods, and 1 at half periods. Negative `t` wraps with floor modulo.

procedures.name: pulse
procedures.summary: Period-1 pulse. Returns 1 while the wrapped phase is below `clamp(duty, 0, 1)`.
procedures.documentation: `pulse(t, duty) -> 0 or 1`. Period-1 pulse. Returns 1 while the wrapped phase is below `clamp(duty, 0, 1)`.

procedures.name: wrapdeg
procedures.summary: Wraps an angle into 0…360, excluding 360.
procedures.documentation: `wrapdeg(d) -> number`. Wraps an angle into 0…360, excluding 360.

procedures.name: angdiff
procedures.summary: Shortest signed rotation from `a` to `b`, in −180…180, excluding +180. Positive is clockwise.
procedures.documentation: `angdiff(a, b) -> number`. Shortest signed rotation from `a` to `b`, in −180…180, excluding +180. Positive is clockwise.

procedures.name: lerpheading
procedures.summary: Interpolates along the shortest angular route and wraps the result. `t` is not clamped.
procedures.documentation: `lerpheading(a, b, t) -> number`. Interpolates along the shortest angular route and wraps the result. `t` is not clamped.

procedures.name: vperp
procedures.summary: Returns `[-v[1], v[0]]`, a 90° mathematical counter-clockwise perpendicular in Cartesian coordinates.
procedures.documentation: `vperp(v) -> point`. Returns `[-v[1], v[0]]`, a 90° mathematical counter-clockwise perpendicular in Cartesian coordinates.

procedures.name: vproj
procedures.summary: Projects vector `a` onto `b`. Returns `[0, 0]` when `b` has near-zero squared length.
procedures.documentation: `vproj(a, b) -> point`. Projects vector `a` onto `b`. Returns `[0, 0]` when `b` has near-zero squared length.

procedures.name: vreflect
procedures.summary: Reflects `v` across the line whose normal is `n`. `n` need not be normalized; a near-zero normal returns a copy of `v`.
procedures.documentation: `vreflect(v, n) -> point`. Reflects `v` across the line whose normal is `n`. `n` need not be normalized; a near-zero normal returns a copy of `v`.

procedures.name: remapc
procedures.summary: Clamped linear remap. Reversed input/output ranges work. A near-zero input span returns `outlo`.
procedures.documentation: `remapc(v, inlo, inhi, outlo, outhi) -> number`. Clamped linear remap. Reversed input/output ranges work. A near-zero input span returns `outlo`.

procedures.name: randbetween
procedures.summary: Uniform value starting at `a` with span `b-a`; consumes **1 draw**. Reversed bounds therefore work.
procedures.documentation: `randbetween(a, b) -> number`. Uniform value starting at `a` with span `b-a`; consumes **1 draw**. Reversed bounds therefore work.

procedures.name: randint
procedures.summary: Uniform inclusive integer between `ceil(min(a,b))` and `floor(max(a,b))`; normally consumes **1 draw**. If the bounds contain no integer, returns the rounded lower bound without drawing.
procedures.documentation: `randint(a, b) -> integer`. Uniform inclusive integer between `ceil(min(a,b))` and `floor(max(a,b))`; normally consumes **1 draw**. If the bounds contain no integer, returns the rounded lower bound without drawing.

procedures.name: chance
procedures.summary: Bernoulli trial with `p` clamped to 0…1; consumes **1 draw** even at probabilities 0 and 1.
procedures.documentation: `chance(p) -> 0 or 1`. Bernoulli trial with `p` clamped to 0…1; consumes **1 draw** even at probabilities 0 and 1.

procedures.name: weightedpick
procedures.summary: Selects from `xs` in order using cumulative weights; consumes **1 draw**. Supply a non-empty `xs`, an equally long `ws`, non-negative weights, and a positive total.
procedures.documentation: `weightedpick(xs, ws) -> value`. Selects from `xs` in order using cumulative weights; consumes **1 draw**. Supply a non-empty `xs`, an equally long `ws`, non-negative weights, and a positive total.

procedures.name: jitterpt
procedures.summary: Independently offsets both coordinates uniformly within `[-mm, mm)`; consumes **2 draws**. Use non-negative `mm`.
procedures.documentation: `jitterpt(p, mm) -> point`. Independently offsets both coordinates uniformly within `[-mm, mm)`; consumes **2 draws**. Use non-negative `mm`.

procedures.name: sortby
procedures.summary: Returns a new list in ascending key order. Computes every key once and leaves `xs` unchanged. Equal-key items keep their original order.
procedures.documentation: `sortby(xs, keyfn) -> list`. Returns a new list in ascending key order. Computes every key once and leaves `xs` unchanged. Equal-key items keep their original order.

procedures.name: argmin
procedures.summary: Returns the first item with the smallest computed key. Keys are computed once. `xs` must be non-empty.
procedures.documentation: `argmin(xs, keyfn) -> value`. Returns the first item with the smallest computed key. Keys are computed once. `xs` must be non-empty.

procedures.name: argmax
procedures.summary: Returns the first item with the largest computed key. Keys are computed once. `xs` must be non-empty.
procedures.documentation: `argmax(xs, keyfn) -> value`. Returns the first item with the largest computed key. Keys are computed once. `xs` must be non-empty.

procedures.name: pairwise
procedures.summary: Returns adjacent pairs: `[a,b,c]` becomes `[[a,b],[b,c]]`. Lists shorter than two produce `[]`.
procedures.documentation: `pairwise(xs) -> list`. Returns adjacent pairs: `[a,b,c]` becomes `[[a,b],[b,c]]`. Lists shorter than two produce `[]`.

procedures.name: zip
procedures.summary: Pairs items at matching indices and stops at the shorter input.
procedures.documentation: `zip(a, b) -> list`. Pairs items at matching indices and stops at the shorter input.

procedures.name: flatten
procedures.summary: Recursively removes all nested list structure while preserving left-to-right leaf order. Empty nested lists contribute nothing.
procedures.documentation: `flatten(xs) -> list`. Recursively removes all nested list structure while preserving left-to-right leaf order. Empty nested lists contribute nothing.

procedures.name: unique
procedures.summary: Removes later duplicates and preserves first occurrence order. Equality follows NeedleScript's deep, tolerant equality rules.
procedures.documentation: `unique(xs) -> list`. Removes later duplicates and preserves first occurrence order. Equality follows NeedleScript's deep, tolerant equality rules.

procedures.name: chunk
procedures.summary: Splits `xs` into consecutive chunks. The width is `max(1, floor(n))`; the last chunk may be shorter.
procedures.documentation: `chunk(xs, n) -> list`. Splits `xs` into consecutive chunks. The width is `max(1, floor(n))`; the last chunk may be shorter.

procedures.name: rotatedlist
procedures.summary: Returns a new list rotated left by `round(n)` places. Negative values rotate right. Empty input returns `[]`.
procedures.documentation: `rotatedlist(xs, n) -> list`. Returns a new list rotated left by `round(n)` places. Negative values rotate right. Empty input returns `[]`.

procedures.name: countif
procedures.summary: Counts items for which the predicate returns non-zero. It has the same predicate requirements as the core `filter`.
procedures.documentation: `countif(xs, predfn) -> number`. Counts items for which the predicate returns non-zero. It has the same predicate requirements as the core `filter`.

procedures.name: polypath
procedures.summary: Regular polygon of radius `r`. Vertex count is `max(3, round(n))`; result length is vertices + 1.
procedures.documentation: `polypath(n, r) -> closed path`. Regular polygon of radius `r`. Vertex count is `max(3, round(n))`; result length is vertices + 1.

procedures.name: starpath
procedures.summary: Alternating outer/inner radii with `max(2, round(n))` points of each kind.
procedures.documentation: `starpath(n, rout, rin) -> closed path`. Alternating outer/inner radii with `max(2, round(n))` points of each kind.

procedures.name: rectpath
procedures.summary: Axis-aligned rectangle of width `w` and height `h`, beginning at the top-edge midpoint.
procedures.documentation: `rectpath(w, h) -> closed path`. Axis-aligned rectangle of width `w` and height `h`, beginning at the top-edge midpoint.

procedures.name: roundrect
procedures.summary: Rounded rectangle with nine samples per corner. Radius is `abs(r)` clamped to half the smaller absolute dimension.
procedures.documentation: `roundrect(w, h, r) -> closed path`. Rounded rectangle with nine samples per corner. Radius is `abs(r)` clamped to half the smaller absolute dimension.

procedures.name: ellipsepath
procedures.summary: Ellipse with 64 perimeter samples; `rx` and `ry` are horizontal and vertical radii.
procedures.documentation: `ellipsepath(rx, ry) -> closed path`. Ellipse with 64 perimeter samples; `rx` and `ry` are horizontal and vertical radii.

procedures.name: arcpath
procedures.summary: Circular arc starting north, sampled at no more than 6° per segment. Positive `deg` progresses counter-clockwise; negative progresses clockwise.
procedures.documentation: `arcpath(deg, r) -> open path`. Circular arc starting north, sampled at no more than 6° per segment. Positive `deg` progresses counter-clockwise; negative progresses clockwise.

procedures.name: coilpath
procedures.summary: Spiral whose radius linearly changes from `r0` to `r1`, with 72 segments per absolute turn. Positive turns progress counter-clockwise.
procedures.documentation: `coilpath(turns, r0, r1) -> open path`. Spiral whose radius linearly changes from `r0` to `r1`, with 72 segments per absolute turn. Positive turns progress counter-clockwise.

procedures.name: heartpath
procedures.summary: Parametric heart with 96 samples. Overall scale is controlled by `size`; the first point is on the north-west lobe.
procedures.documentation: `heartpath(size) -> closed path`. Parametric heart with 96 samples. Overall scale is controlled by `size`; the first point is on the north-west lobe.

procedures.name: gearpath
procedures.summary: Four vertices per tooth, alternating two outer points at `r` and two root points at `max(0, r-depth)`. Uses at least three teeth.
procedures.documentation: `gearpath(teeth, r, depth) -> closed path`. Four vertices per tooth, alternating two outer points at `r` and two root points at `max(0, r-depth)`. Uses at least three teeth.

procedures.name: superellipsepath
procedures.summary: 96-sample superellipse within `w × h`. Exponent `e` is floored at 0.01 before deriving the signed-power curve.
procedures.documentation: `superellipsepath(w, h, e) -> closed path`. 96-sample superellipse within `w × h`. Exponent `e` is floored at 0.01 before deriving the signed-power curve.

procedures.name: wavepath
procedures.summary: Horizontal sine wave from `-length/2` to `length/2`, with 24 segments per absolute cycle. Negative cycles reverse phase progression.
procedures.documentation: `wavepath(length, amp, cycles) -> open path`. Horizontal sine wave from `-length/2` to `length/2`, with 24 segments per absolute cycle. Negative cycles reverse phase progression.

procedures.name: rosepath
procedures.summary: Polar rose `radius = cos(k × angle) × r`, sampled with at least 72 points and 72 per absolute `k`. Integer `k` produces the expected closed rose.
procedures.documentation: `rosepath(k, r) -> closed path`. Polar rose `radius = cos(k × angle) × r`, sampled with at least 72 points and 72 per absolute `k`. Integer `k` produces the expected closed rose.

procedures.name: lissajouspath
procedures.summary: Lissajous curve with x phase in degrees, within a square of side `size`; sample count is at least 96 and scales with `max(abs(a), abs(b))`.
procedures.documentation: `lissajouspath(a, b, phase, size) -> closed path`. Lissajous curve with x phase in degrees, within a square of side `size`; sample count is at least 96 and scales with `max(abs(a), abs(b))`.

procedures.name: pointat
procedures.summary: Point at normalized arc length `t`. A one-point path returns that point; repeated zero-length segments are tolerated.
procedures.documentation: `pointat(path, t) -> point`. Point at normalized arc length `t`. A one-point path returns that point; repeated zero-length segments are tolerated.

procedures.name: headingat
procedures.summary: Heading of the non-zero segment containing `t`. At an exact vertex it selects the preceding segment. If no non-zero segment exists, returns 0.
procedures.documentation: `headingat(path, t) -> heading`. Heading of the non-zero segment containing `t`. At an exact vertex it selects the preceding segment. If no non-zero segment exists, returns 0.

procedures.name: paramof
procedures.summary: Normalized arc-length position of the closest point on the polyline. Ties keep the earlier segment; a zero-length path returns 0.
procedures.documentation: `paramof(p, path) -> number`. Normalized arc-length position of the closest point on the polyline. Ties keep the earlier segment; a zero-length path returns 0.

procedures.name: subpath
procedures.summary: Extracts a section, including interpolated endpoints and interior original vertices. If `t1 < t0`, returns the forward extraction reversed. Equal parameters return two equal endpoints.
procedures.documentation: `subpath(path, t0, t1) -> path`. Extracts a section, including interpolated endpoints and interior original vertices. If `t1 < t0`, returns the forward extraction reversed. Equal parameters return two equal endpoints.

procedures.name: dashes
procedures.summary: Splits an arc-length route into on-segments. `phasemm` enters that far into the repeating cycle and may begin in a dash or gap. Use non-negative lengths and a positive sum.
procedures.documentation: `dashes(path, onmm, offmm[, phasemm]) -> list of paths`. Splits an arc-length route into on-segments. `phasemm` enters that far into the repeating cycle and may begin in a dash or gap. Use non-negative lengths and a positive sum.

procedures.name: simplifypath
procedures.summary: Ramer–Douglas–Peucker simplification using perpendicular segment distance. Negative tolerance becomes 0; endpoints are preserved.
procedures.documentation: `simplifypath(path, tol) -> path`. Ramer–Douglas–Peucker simplification using perpendicular segment distance. Negative tolerance becomes 0; endpoints are preserved.

procedures.name: smoothclosed
procedures.summary: Applies 0…6 rounded Chaikin corner-cutting passes. An existing duplicate closing point is removed first, then one closing point is appended. Each pass doubles the unique point count.
procedures.documentation: `smoothclosed(ring, n) -> closed path`. Applies 0…6 rounded Chaikin corner-cutting passes. An existing duplicate closing point is removed first, then one closing point is appended. Each pass doubles the unique point count.

procedures.name: morphpaths
procedures.summary: Arc-length-resamples both paths to the larger unique-point count and linearly interpolates corresponding points. `t` is not clamped. The result is closed only if both inputs are closed.
procedures.documentation: `morphpaths(a, b, t) -> path`. Arc-length-resamples both paths to the larger unique-point count and linearly interpolates corresponding points. `t` is not clamped. The result is closed only if both inputs are closed.

procedures.name: pathisects
procedures.summary: Returns unique segment intersections in nested segment order. Collinear overlap behavior follows core `segisect`.
procedures.documentation: `pathisects(a, b) -> list of points`. Returns unique segment intersections in nested segment order. Collinear overlap behavior follows core `segisect`.

procedures.name: offsetopen
procedures.summary: Approximate mitered offset of an open polyline. Positive `mm` offsets to the path's Cartesian left; negative offsets right. Near-180° joins use a bounded denominator to avoid division by zero.
procedures.documentation: `offsetopen(path, mm) -> path`. Approximate mitered offset of an open polyline. Positive `mm` offsets to the path's Cartesian left; negative offsets right. Near-180° joins use a bounded denominator to avoid division by zero.

procedures.name: regionarea
procedures.summary: Absolute shoelace area in mm². Orientation does not affect the result.
procedures.documentation: `regionarea(region) -> number`. Absolute shoelace area in mm². Orientation does not affect the result.

procedures.name: poleof
procedures.summary: Deterministic approximation of the interior point farthest from the boundary. It tests the centroid, a 9×9 bounding-box grid, then seven local refinements. Useful for labels and seed points; it is not an exact polylabel solution.
procedures.documentation: `poleof(region) -> point`. Deterministic approximation of the interior point farthest from the boundary. It tests the centroid, a 9×9 bounding-box grid, then seven local refinements. Useful for labels and seed points; it is not an exact polylabel solution.

procedures.name: insetrings
procedures.summary: Repeatedly offsets inward by `abs(gap)`, returning every piece from levels 1 through `max(0, round(n))`. Splits and collapsed levels are handled by `offsetpath`; the original region is not included.
procedures.documentation: `insetrings(region, gap, n) -> list of regions`. Repeatedly offsets inward by `abs(gap)`, returning every piece from levels 1 through `max(0, round(n))`. Splits and collapsed levels are handled by `offsetpath`; the original region is not included.

procedures.name: tilecells
procedures.summary: Covers and clips a global grid of cells to the region. `kind` must be `'square'`, `'hex'`, or `'tri'`; `cell` must be positive. Hex `cell` is circumradius; triangular cells are halves of square cells. Boundary cells may be partial or split.
procedures.documentation: `tilecells(region, kind, cell) -> list of regions`. Covers and clips a global grid of cells to the region. `kind` must be `'square'`, `'hex'`, or `'tri'`; `cell` must be positive. Hex `cell` is circumradius; triangular cells are halves of square cells. Boundary cells may be partial or split.

procedures.name: gridpoints
procedures.summary: Returns centers of globally aligned `cell × cell` boxes that lie inside the region. `cell` must be positive. Points on the upper/right incomplete fringe are not sampled.
procedures.documentation: `gridpoints(region, cell) -> list of points`. Returns centers of globally aligned `cell × cell` boxes that lie inside the region. `cell` must be positive. Points on the upper/right incomplete fringe are not sampled.

procedures.name: partitions
procedures.summary: Produces `max(1, round(n))` clipped Voronoi cells after two centroidal-relaxation passes. Initial seeds use `scatter`, with grid/pole fallbacks. Consumes exactly **1 main-stream RNG draw**, regardless of the number of generated seeds.
procedures.documentation: `partitions(region, n) -> list of regions`. Produces `max(1, round(n))` clipped Voronoi cells after two centroidal-relaxation passes. Initial seeds use `scatter`, with grid/pole fallbacks. Consumes exactly **1 main-stream RNG draw**, regardless of the number of generated seeds.

procedures.name: circlelayout
procedures.summary: Returns `max(0, round(n))` evenly spaced positions on radius `r`. The first is north. Each heading is tangent to the circle in counter-clockwise traversal; zero count returns `[]`.
procedures.documentation: `circlelayout(n, r) -> placements`. Returns `max(0, round(n))` evenly spaced positions on radius `r`. The first is north. Each heading is tangent to the circle in counter-clockwise traversal; zero count returns `[]`.

procedures.name: gridlayout
procedures.summary: Centered row-major grid with rounded non-negative dimensions. Starts at the top-left for positive spacing; every heading is 0. Negative spacing mirrors an axis.
procedures.documentation: `gridlayout(cols, rows, dx, dy) -> placements`. Centered row-major grid with rounded non-negative dimensions. Starts at the top-left for positive spacing; every heading is 0. Negative spacing mirrors an axis.

procedures.name: alongpath
procedures.summary: Returns rounded non-negative count at equal normalized arc-length parameters, including both ends. One placement uses the midpoint (`t = 0.5`). Headings follow `std.pathops.headingat`.
procedures.documentation: `alongpath(path, n) -> placements`. Returns rounded non-negative count at equal normalized arc-length parameters, including both ends. One placement uses the midpoint (`t = 0.5`). Headings follow `std.pathops.headingat`.

procedures.name: fitpath
procedures.summary: Uniformly scales `path` to fit the region's bounding box after a non-negative margin, then centers bounding boxes. Preserves aspect ratio and handles horizontal, vertical, and point-like source paths. It fits the bounding box, not the exact polygon interior.
procedures.documentation: `fitpath(path, region, margin) -> path`. Uniformly scales `path` to fit the region's bounding box after a non-negative margin, then centers bounding boxes. Preserves aspect ratio and handles horizontal, vertical, and point-like source paths. It fits the bounding box, not the exact polygon interior.

procedures.name: radialdir
procedures.summary: Heading of the ray from origin to `p`; returns 0 within `0.000001` mm of the origin.
procedures.documentation: `radialdir(p) -> heading`. Heading of the ray from origin to `p`; returns 0 within `0.000001` mm of the origin.

procedures.name: griddir
procedures.summary: Returns a direction reporter that ignores its point and always returns `deg`. Example: `fill dir griddir(30)`.
procedures.documentation: `griddir(deg) -> reference`. Returns a direction reporter that ignores its point and always returns `deg`. Example: `fill dir griddir(30)`.

procedures.name: radialdirfrom
procedures.summary: Returns a reporter whose rays originate at `[cx, cy]`; returns 0 at that center.
procedures.documentation: `radialdirfrom(cx, cy) -> reference`. Returns a reporter whose rays originate at `[cx, cy]`; returns 0 at that center.

procedures.name: curldir
procedures.summary: Divergence-free direction derived from finite differences of simplex noise at a fixed 14 mm scale. Returns 0 for a near-zero gradient.
procedures.documentation: `curldir(p) -> heading`. Divergence-free direction derived from finite differences of simplex noise at a fixed 14 mm scale. Returns 0 for a near-zero gradient.

procedures.name: curldirwith
procedures.summary: Configurable form of `curldir`; `scaledown` is the spatial noise scale and must be positive. Larger values vary more slowly.
procedures.documentation: `curldirwith(scaledown) -> reference`. Configurable form of `curldir`; `scaledown` is the spatial noise scale and must be positive. Larger values vary more slowly.

procedures.name: wovenshape
procedures.summary: Uses 0.8 mm row spacing, 3 mm stitches, and alternates phase 0/0.5 by row parity for a woven rhythm.
procedures.documentation: `wovenshape(p, row, v) -> descriptor`. Uses 0.8 mm row spacing, 3 mm stitches, and alternates phase 0/0.5 by row parity for a woven rhythm.

procedures.name: gradientshape
procedures.summary: Ramps row spacing from 0.45 to 1.2 mm using clamped cross-field coordinate `v`; stitch length is 2.5 mm and phase 0.5.
procedures.documentation: `gradientshape(p, row, v) -> descriptor`. Ramps row spacing from 0.45 to 1.2 mm using clamped cross-field coordinate `v`; stitch length is 2.5 mm and phase 0.5.

procedures.name: gradientshapewith
procedures.summary: Configurable gradient reporter interpolating spacing from `lo` to `hi`; it does not clamp the supplied spacing endpoints.
procedures.documentation: `gradientshapewith(lo, hi) -> reference`. Configurable gradient reporter interpolating spacing from `lo` to `hi`; it does not clamp the supplied spacing endpoints.

procedures.name: hilbertpaths
procedures.summary: Builds the smallest power-of-two Hilbert grid whose scaled span covers the larger bounding-box dimension, then clips its continuous curve. `cell` controls target detail and must be positive.
procedures.documentation: `hilbertpaths(region, cell) -> paths`. Builds the smallest power-of-two Hilbert grid whose scaled span covers the larger bounding-box dimension, then clips its continuous curve. `cell` controls target detail and must be positive.

procedures.name: truchetpaths
procedures.summary: Alternating checkerboard Truchet quarter-circles, sampled every 15°. `cell` is tile size and must be positive.
procedures.documentation: `truchetpaths(region, cell) -> paths`. Alternating checkerboard Truchet quarter-circles, sampled every 15°. `cell` is tile size and must be positive.

procedures.name: hitomezashi
procedures.summary: Alternating horizontal and vertical sashiko dashes. Rounded bit values modulo 2 set row and column phases cyclically, including at negative grid indices. `cell` must be positive and both bit lists non-empty.
procedures.documentation: `hitomezashi(region, cell, rowbits, colbits) -> paths`. Alternating horizontal and vertical sashiko dashes. Rounded bit values modulo 2 set row and column phases cyclically, including at negative grid indices. `cell` must be positive and both bit lists non-empty.

procedures.name: seigaiha
procedures.summary: Staggered Japanese wave pattern with three concentric semicircles at each origin. `r` is the largest radius and must be positive.
procedures.documentation: `seigaiha(region, r) -> paths`. Staggered Japanese wave pattern with three concentric semicircles at each origin. `r` is the largest radius and must be positive.

procedures.name: asanoha
procedures.summary: Hexagonally arranged hemp-leaf spokes and half-edges. `cell` must be positive.
procedures.documentation: `asanoha(region, cell) -> paths`. Hexagonally arranged hemp-leaf spokes and half-edges. `cell` must be positive.

procedures.name: herringbonepaths
procedures.summary: Staggered zigzag herringbone units with horizontal/vertical scale `w`, which must be positive.
procedures.documentation: `herringbonepaths(region, w) -> paths`. Staggered zigzag herringbone units with horizontal/vertical scale `w`, which must be positive.

procedures.name: sewrun
procedures.summary: Resamples `path` at spacing `mm`, then sews it with the current stitch mode and thread. Equivalent to `sewpath(resample(path, mm))`.
procedures.documentation: `sewrun(path, mm)`. Resamples `path` at spacing `mm`, then sews it with the current stitch mode and thread. Equivalent to `sewpath(resample(path, mm))`.

procedures.name: satinalong
procedures.summary: Enables satin width `w`, sews `path`, then sets satin width to 0. The final satin state is always off, not restored to a prior width. Other satin settings still apply.
procedures.documentation: `satinalong(path, w)`. Enables satin width `w`, sews `path`, then sets satin width to 0. The final satin state is always off, not restored to a prior width. Other satin settings still apply.

procedures.name: beanoutline
procedures.summary: Enables bean repeat `n`, sews the logically closed region, then sets bean repeat to 1. The prior bean setting is not restored.
procedures.documentation: `beanoutline(region, n)`. Enables bean repeat `n`, sews the logically closed region, then sets bean repeat to 1. The prior bean setting is not restored.

procedures.name: appliquesteps
procedures.summary: Performs a 2.5 mm running placement line, a narrow satin tack-down at `max(0.8, 0.35w)`, and a final satin cover at `w`. Inserts `stop` events between the three stages so fabric can be placed/trimmed. Each stage travels with needle up to the ring start. Ends at the closed ring's end with satin turned off.
procedures.documentation: `appliquesteps(region, w)`. Performs a 2.5 mm running placement line, a narrow satin tack-down at `max(0.8, 0.35w)`, and a final satin cover at `w`. Inserts `stop` events between the three stages so fabric can be placed/trimmed. Each stage travels with needle up to the ring start. Ends at the closed ring's end with satin turned off.

procedures.name: appliquewith
procedures.summary: Configurable three-stage appliqué construction. See below.
procedures.documentation: `appliquewith(region, placementInset, tackdownInset, coverWidth, stops)`. Configurable three-stage appliqué construction. See below.

procedures.name: eyelet
procedures.summary: Sews a resampled satin circle centered at the current needle position. Radius must be positive; satin width is `clamp(0.55r, 0.6, 1.5)`. A `push`/`pop` pair restores needle position, heading, and pen state after sewing; satin ends off.
procedures.documentation: `eyelet(r)`. Sews a resampled satin circle centered at the current needle position. Radius must be positive; satin width is `clamp(0.55r, 0.6, 1.5)`. A `push`/`pop` pair restores needle position, heading, and pen state after sewing; satin ends off.

procedures.name: fillbordergeometry
procedures.summary: Pure fill-and-border construction geometry. See below.
procedures.documentation: `fillbordergeometry(region, coverWidth, overlap) -> [fillRings, borderPaths, inset]`. Pure fill-and-border construction geometry. See below.

procedures.name: fillandborder
procedures.summary: Sews inset fill rows, inserts a `stop`, then sews the satin border. Uses the standard 0.4 mm overlap.
procedures.documentation: `fillandborder(region, deg, spacing, coverWidth)`. Sews inset fill rows, inserts a `stop`, then sews the satin border. Uses the standard 0.4 mm overlap.

procedures.name: fillandborderwith
procedures.summary: Explicit-overlap form of `fillandborder`.
procedures.documentation: `fillandborderwith(region, deg, spacing, coverWidth, overlap)`. Explicit-overlap form of `fillandborder`.

procedures.name: gradientbands
procedures.summary: Geometry-only helper: slices a region into `max(1, round(n))` parallel bands oriented at heading/angle `deg` and returns all clipped pieces in band order. Concavity can yield more pieces than requested bands.
procedures.documentation: `gradientbands(region, deg, n) -> list of regions`. Geometry-only helper: slices a region into `max(1, round(n))` parallel bands oriented at heading/angle `deg` and returns all clipped pieces in band order. Concavity can yield more pieces than requested bands.

procedures.name: gradientrows
procedures.summary: Geometry-only, density-neutral two-color blend. See below.
procedures.documentation: `gradientrows(region, deg, pitch, amount) -> [rowsA, rowsB]`. Geometry-only, density-neutral two-color blend. See below.

procedures.name: gradientrowsn
procedures.summary: Geometry-only, density-neutral blend across 2–8 colors. See below.
procedures.documentation: `gradientrowsn(region, deg, pitch, weights) -> list of row groups`. Geometry-only, density-neutral blend across 2–8 colors. See below.

procedures.name: serpentinerows
procedures.summary: Greedily routes parallel row paths with endpoint reversal enabled, beginning from the first row when `reversed` is false or the last row when true. Returns `[]` for empty input and does not mutate `rows`.
procedures.documentation: `serpentinerows(rows, reversed) -> routed rows`. Greedily routes parallel row paths with endpoint reversal enabled, beginning from the first row when `reversed` is false or the last row when true. Returns `[]` for empty input and does not mutate `rows`.

procedures.name: knockdown
procedures.summary: Sparse running-stitch foundation for fleece, terry, and other high-pile fabrics. See below.
procedures.documentation: `knockdown(region, deg, spacing)`. Sparse running-stitch foundation for fleece, terry, and other high-pile fabrics. See below.

procedures.name: threadblend
procedures.summary: Creates 1.2 mm fill rows at `deg`, sews even rows in the current color, advances once to the next color, then sews odd rows. Rows are resampled at 2.5 mm. Ends in the second color and does not restore needle position.
procedures.documentation: `threadblend(region, deg)`. Creates 1.2 mm fill rows at `deg`, sews even rows in the current color, advances once to the next color, then sews odd rows. Rows are resampled at 2.5 mm. Ends in the second color and does not restore needle position.

procedures.name: stipple
procedures.summary: Scatters candidate points and sews a small circular mark only where coverage within `mindist/3` is below one layer. `mindist` must be positive. Each mark restores turtle state with `push`/`pop`. Consumes exactly **1 main-stream RNG draw** through `scatter`.
procedures.documentation: `stipple(region, mindist)`. Scatters candidate points and sews a small circular mark only where coverage within `mindist/3` is below one layer. `mindist` must be positive. Each mark restores turtle state with `push`/`pop`. Consumes exactly **1 main-stream RNG draw** through `scatter`.

procedures.name: chalkgrid
procedures.summary: Adds a `'grid'` line group spanning the configured field bounds, aligned to global multiples of `cell`. `cell` must be positive.
procedures.documentation: `chalkgrid(cell)`. Adds a `'grid'` line group spanning the configured field bounds, aligned to global multiples of `cell`. `cell` must be positive.

procedures.name: chalkbbox
procedures.summary: Adds a closed, axis-aligned `'bbox'` line overlay around `path`. Expects non-empty geometry accepted by core `bbox`.
procedures.documentation: `chalkbbox(path)`. Adds a closed, axis-aligned `'bbox'` line overlay around `path`. Expects non-empty geometry accepted by core `bbox`.

procedures.name: chalkfield
procedures.summary: Adds a `'field'` line overlay of the current sewable field path. Works for circular and rectangular hoop fields.
procedures.documentation: `chalkfield()`. Adds a `'field'` line overlay of the current sewable field path. Works for circular and rectangular hoop fields.

procedures.name: threadestimate
procedures.summary: Returns the polyline length through committed penetration points, in millimetres, or 0 with fewer than two points. It is an estimate: stitch history does not retain trims or color boundaries, and it does not model bobbin/thread consumption.
procedures.documentation: `threadestimate() -> number`. Returns the polyline length through committed penetration points, in millimetres, or 0 with fewer than two points. It is an estimate: stitch history does not retain trims or color boundaries, and it does not model bobbin/thread consumption.

procedures.name: coverprofile
procedures.summary: Samples `coverat` along a resampled path and returns `[distanceMm, coverageLayers]` pairs. `stride` must be positive. Empty path returns `[]`; a one-point path returns one sample at distance 0.
procedures.documentation: `coverprofile(path, stride) -> list`. Samples `coverat` along a resampled path and returns `[distanceMm, coverageLayers]` pairs. `stride` must be positive. Empty path returns `[]`; a one-point path returns one sample at distance 0.
